case_name: Mordel  v Royal Berkshire NHS Foundation Trust [2019] EWHC 2591 (QB) (08 October 2019)

summary.brief: Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Aleksander Mordel was born to the claimant on 25 th January 2015 with Trisomy 21 or Down's syndrome.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On 23 rd June 2014 the claimant attended the community midwife at her GP's surgery for her booking appointment and 'accepted' all six of the standard screening tests, including combined screening.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On 22 nd July 2014 a maternal ultrasound report was completed by Ms Lorraine Bracher, the reporting sonographer. This provided, insofar as is material: 'Down's screening declined'.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr Chudleigh said that this was insufficient follow-up because she would want to explore the patient's reasons, as she put it, including any possible misunderstanding as to the purpose of the test.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: I am constrained to hold that Midwife Foley failed to discharge her duty to the claimant on 11 th August 2014 in not exploring why the combined test had not been carried out.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: There must be judgment for the claimant.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: First pregnancy, Polish as first language

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient attended for routine antenatal screening and was booked for Down's syndrome screening.

requestables.name: Nuchal Translucency Ultrasound
requestables.description: Ultrasound to measure nuchal translucency for Down's syndrome screening

reveal.result_summary: Not performed, marked as 'Down's screening declined'
reveal.clinical_significance: Missed opportunity for early Down's syndrome detection
reveal.decision_impact: Should have prompted further discussion and informed consent

requestables.name: Combined Screening Blood Test
requestables.description: Blood test for Beta hCG and PAPP-P as part of Down's syndrome screening

reveal.clinical_significance: Part of combined screening for Down's syndrome
reveal.decision_impact: Should have been performed if screening was accepted

timeline_phases.name: Initial Antenatal Booking
timeline_phases.description: Patient attends initial antenatal booking appointment

timeline_phases.name: Scheduled Down's Syndrome Screening
timeline_phases.description: Patient scheduled for Down's syndrome screening

key_events.event: Ultrasound appointment for Down's syndrome screening
Midwife did not explore screening decision

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient attending for Down's syndrome screening
decision_points.prompt: How should the sonographer confirm the patient's consent for Down's syndrome screening?

options.description: Confirm understanding and consent for screening with detailed explanation
options.clinical_reasoning: Ensures informed consent and understanding of the procedure

options.description: Proceed with ultrasound without confirming consent
options.clinical_reasoning: Assumes consent without verification, leading to potential misunderstanding

actual_action_defendant.description: Sonographer marked 'Down's screening declined' without confirming consent
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Assumed patient declined screening based on brief interaction

expected_action_court.description: Sonographer should have confirmed consent with detailed explanation
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Informed consent requires clear communication and understanding

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failed to ensure informed consent, leading to missed screening opportunity
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Detailed discussion to confirm patient's understanding and consent
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Montgomery v Lanarkshire Health Board principles on informed consent

decision_points.prompt: What should the midwife have done regarding the Down's screening?

options.description: Discuss and confirm patient's wishes regarding Down's screening
options.clinical_reasoning: Ensures patient's wishes are respected and documented

options.description: Assume patient's decision from ultrasound report
options.clinical_reasoning: Relies on previous documentation without confirmation

actual_action_defendant.description: Midwife did not explore the reason for declined screening

expected_action_court.description: Midwife should have confirmed patient's wishes

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failed to confirm patient's wishes at follow-up
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Midwife should have discussed screening options

key_findings: Failure to confirm informed consent for Down's screening
Failure to explore patient's wishes at follow-up

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to confirm consent and explore wishes
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper consent and follow-up would have led to different outcome

factual_timeline.event: Patient attended booking appointment with midwife
Patient attended ultrasound appointment, screening marked as declined

allegations.allegation: Failure to obtain informed consent for Down's syndrome screening

court_findings.finding: Defendant failed to obtain informed consent
court_findings.legal_significance: Breach of duty under Montgomery principles
